channel.title: Java on Computer Worlds
channel.description: Recent content in Java on Computer Worlds

item.title: 01 Classes and Objects
item.description: Key Concepts object, class, instance, method, signature, parameter, type, state, source code, return value, compiler Java objects model objects from a problem domain. Objects are created from classes. The class describes the kind of object; the objects represent individual instances of the class We can communicate with objects by invoking methods on them. Objects usually do something if we invoke a method. Methods can have parameters to provide additional information for a task. The method name and the parameter types of a method are called its signature.

item.title: 03 Object interactions
item.description: Key Ideas Abstraction is the ability to ignore details of parts to focus attention on a higher level of a problem. Modularization is the process of dividing a whole into well-defined parts, which can be built and examined separately, and which interact in well-defined ways. The class diagram shows the classes of an applica-tion and the relationships between them. It gives infor-mation about the source code and presents the static view of a program.
